subject: Public Health
title: Integrated Management of Childhood Illness
description: A short lesson to help you apply Integrated Management of Childhood Illness.

hook.text: Children rarely die from one cause alone—pneumonia, diarrhea, and malnutrition overlap. IMCI treats the whole child.

buildup.text: Developed by WHO and UNICEF, IMCI trains health workers to assess and treat the most common childhood killers together.

discovery.text: IMCI combines clinical guidelines, health system strengthening, and community health education into one strategy.

twist.text: Countries implementing IMCI saw child mortality drop by up to 50%—through better case management, not new technology.

climax.text: Updating IMCI for the digital age—using mobile apps and decision-support tools—could extend its impact further.

punchline.text: Treat the child, not just the disease—that's the principle that saves millions.
